title: Creamy British Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh, herb‑laden salad featuring sautéed mushrooms tossed in a silky red‑wine‑vinegar cream dressing, perfect for a crowd‑pleasing British lunch.

ingredients.name: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Wash all salad leaves thoroughly and pat dry; place them in a large bowl.
Clean the mushrooms with a damp paper towel and slice them into even pieces.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add a splash of olive oil.
Add the sliced mushrooms, shallot, and garlic; sauté for about 6–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms are golden and the shallot is translucent.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool slightly.
In a small bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, chopped parsley, chives, thyme, and a pinch of salt and pepper until well combined.
Fold the sautéed mushrooms into the dressing, ensuring even coating.
Toss the dressed mushrooms with the prepared salad leaves, season with additional salt and pepper if needed, and serve immediately.
